rulings.Case Title: VALERIE ANN CARLING et al VS. ALBAY CONSTRUCTION COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Be Relieved As Counsel- Civil
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 4, 2008, line 1. Counsel for PLAINTIFFS VALERIE CARLING AND PENNI WHITE'S MOTION To Be Relieved As Counsel. A hearing is required. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: JOANNE LOZENSKI et al VS. SAN FRANCISCO POLICE DEPARTMENT et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 4, 2008, line 2. DEFENDANTS ALEX FAGAN, ROD NAKANISHI AND CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The Court adopts its tentative ruling of August 14, 2008 with these changes. The Court declines to rule on Defendants' objections to "Defs.' UMF 9, 17, 24, 32, 37-39 and defendants' objections to plaintiffs' statement of facts. The objections do not comply with Cal. Rule of Court 3.1354. The Court has ruled on the objections that do comply in parts II - IV of the Parties' Objections filed on August 26, 2008. It remains the Court's view that the motion as to Officer Stockwell is denied based on there being triable issues of fact related Officer Stockwell being brought up on charges for failing to discharge her responsibilities as Station Keeper. The motion is granted as to Officer Rolovich who has failed to present evidence that creates a triable issue of fact that the reason for her suspension from the overtime program was pretextual. Officer Greely's declaration sets forth the relevant undisputed facts. (DUMF 33, 34 and 36.) Because the Court has declined to rule on defendants' objections directed to the evidence presented as to Officer Lozenski, the Court finds there are triable issues of fact related to her claims. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: MANUEL MUNOZ-ALFARO VS. D.N. SECURITY SERVICES, LLC, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing For Application For Attorney'S Fees And Costs;Application
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 4, 2008, line 5. PLAINTIFF MANUEL MUNOZ-ALFARO'S Application For Attorney's Fees And Costs. A hearing is required to address the need for an evidentiary record, the terms of the settlement and the circumstances leading to the dismissal of the class action. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: MAN-LI HUANG VS. CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER, A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Tax Costs;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 4, 2008, line 6. PLAINTIFF MAN-LI HUANG'S Motion To Tax Costs is granted as to $72 for filing and motion fees; $1,100 for cancelled deposition; $68.26 for meals; $1,383 for "service of process" (messenger fees) and $508 for Court Reporter fee. Deny in all other respects. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: MAN-LI HUANG VS. CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER, A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorney'S Fees;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 4, 2008, line 6. DEFENDANTS CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER, KARMI OBERWAGER, LIN VELASQUEZ AND ELIZABETH AVANTS' Motion For Attorney's Fees. The motion is denied. Plaintiff had a prima facie basis for the retaliation claim which was viable until the decision Jones v. The Lodge at Torrey Pines (2008) 42 Cal. 4th 1158. Plaintiff's other claims were linked to the retaliation claim. Plaintiff was unable to present substantial responsive evidence demonstrating a retaliatory animus, but that is not a basis for awarding fees. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: JOEL ASSEKO et al VS. GUARDSMARK GP LLC et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 4, 2008, line 8. DEFENDANT GUARDSMARK LLC'S DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES BYRNE VS. NEGUESSE ZAWDIE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc And Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 4, 2008, line 9. DEFENDANT NAGUSSE ZAWDIE'S Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment is granted. Although the parties offer conflicting declarations on many points, certain facts are not disputed. Defendant was out of the country when the lien was perfected and communications ceased at that point. Plaintiff did not notify defendant's counsel when seeking the default. The law favors a trial on the merits. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: LEAF FUNDING, INC., A CORPORATION VS. GENE L TYGIELSKI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Relief From Default Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 4, 2008, line 11. DEFENDANTS GENE TYGIELSKI, SAMIR KHOURY'S Motion For Relief From Default Judgment. The motion of Tygielski is denied. He had notice of the suit and the facts alleged do not establish excusable neglect. The motion of Khoury is granted. His illness prevented him from responding to the complaint. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: MARIA PLASCENCIA et al VS. LONHYN JASINSKYJ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hearing On Df'S Petition To Compel Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 4, 2008, line 12. DEFENDANT LONHYN JASINSKYJ'S Petition To Compel Arbitration is denied. The pending action involves multiple parties and if the Court were to grant this petition, there is a possibility of conflicting rules on common issues of law and fact. CC 1281.2; Cronus Investments, Inc. v. Concierge Services (2005) 35 Cal. 4th 376, 393. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: RUKHSANA TASNEEN VS. HERMAN AUTRY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Vacate Default And Default Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 4, 2008, line 13. DEFENDANT HERMAN AUTRY'S Motion To Vacate Default And Default Judgment is granted. No opposition filed. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: WOO JA IM DHONG PANETTE VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings, Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 4, 2008, line 14. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings is denied based on the decision in Blair v. Superior Court (1990) 218 Cal. App. 3d 221. Plaintiff's notice made specific reference to the door of the bus closing on her leg. As a consequence, the City was on notice that the door was involved as well as the conduct of the driver. =(302/PJM/VC)
